title: DDR Biomarker & Drug Response Pipeline
question: Which DDR mutations predict sensitivity to PARP and ATR inhibitors?
dataset_target: GDSC2 release 8.5 + DepMap mutation profiles + ATR inhibitor coverage (VE-821, AZD6738)
dataset_actual_poc: GDSC v17 (988 cell lines, IC50 + binary mutation matrix via gdsctools)
substitution_reason: cog.sanger.ac.uk release 8.5 not reachable; v17 is the accessible bundle. ATR inhibitors VE-821 and AZD6738 are absent from v17 — KU-55933 (ATM) and AZD7762 (CHK) used as DDR-pathway controls.
reproduce_command: pip install pandas numpy scipy scikit-learn shap matplotlib gdsctools && python scripts/poc/run_poc.py

headline_result.feature_block: 11 DDR genes retained from a 26-gene panel + MSI + tissue covariates
headline_result.tissue_effect: Adding tissue context lifts per-drug CV AUC from 0.52–0.60 to 0.68–0.77 — most of the discriminative signal is lineage-driven
headline_result.msi_subgroup_finding: MSI-H cell lines preferentially sensitive to ATM (KU-55933 MWU p=0.022) and CHK (AZD7762 MWU p=0.019) inhibitors — independent of HRD score
headline_result.honest_assessment: Direction of HRD–IC50 correlation is positive (more mutations → higher IC50, more resistant), reflecting confounding by general cancer-driver burden (TP53, etc.) rather than DDR-specific synthetic lethality

limitations: Mutation-only HRD is a crude proxy; published HRD scar scores (HRD-LOH, LST, TAI, Myriad) typically perform better
15 of 26 intended DDR genes are unavailable in v17 (PALB2, RAD51 family, BARD1, BRIP1, NBN, MRE11, RAD50, FANC family)
VE-821 and AZD6738 (ATRi) are in GDSC 8.x but not v17 — ATR sensitivity is not directly assessed
Tissue covariate is doing most of the work in the 0.68–0.77 AUC range; DDR/MSI contribute less than tissue alone in several drugs
Cell-line analysis only — no patient cohort validation
role: DDR/synthetic-lethality framing from thesis ARID1A finding; feature construction around HRD biology; biological coherence review of outputs. Implementation AI-assisted.
